stem: A right rectangular prism has a base area of 30s square centimeters (cm²). The length of the base of the rectangular prism is 5/2 cm, and the height of the rectangular prism is 12 cm. Which expression represents the surface area, in cm², of the right rectangular prism?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. The width of the base is (base area)/(length) = 30s/(5/2) = 12s cm. The surface area of a rectangular prism is 2(LW + LH + WH) = 2(30s + (5/2)(12) + (12s)(12)) = 2(30s + 30 + 144s) = 2(174s + 30) = 348s + 60.

Choice B omits doubling. Choice C miscomputes the lateral area. Choice D uses only one pair of faces.
